steps.title: Use the discovery contrast
steps.content: The word but contrasts Theia's earlier status with the new claim that pieces of it have been found in Earth's mantle. Before that claimed physical evidence, Theia existed as a theory or concept.

steps.title: Reject the wrong descriptions
steps.content: Choice A means lacking a plan or connection. Choice B means false or not genuine, but the passage does not say the theory was fraudulent. Choice C means real, which clashes with the later discovery contrast. The answer is D.
